meaning: vi.为琐事费心,拘泥小节vt.小气地给...
examples: The life we have built together is more important than any minor niggle either of us might have.
Okay, there's a niggle or two, but generally the IT folks can sort it out.
We had tremendous difficulty getting the agreement signed Every clause was niggle over.
If we have one niggle, it's that the main volume switches remind us of the cheap volume dials found on lower-end headsets.
This editor did experience cartilage cramps near the backs of his ears after an hour or so of wearing the cans, however -- a niggle at best.
